skill.name: session-init
skill.description: Starts a Claude Code or Codex session by checking git status, task files, project rules, and optional Claude-mem context.
skill.summary: Prepares a work session with project status, active tasks, and next-action guidance.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the skill grants Bash and documents local shell commands for session inspection. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, credential exfiltration, weak cryptography, or obfuscated payload was found. Publish with a warning because it reads workspace files and a hidden Claude-mem settings file.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Based Session Inspection
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents shell commands for wc, git, cat, and head during session startup. The commands are local and mostly hardcoded, but they require external command execution and can reveal repository state and task files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash command examples are explicit and semantically tied to the skill workflow. Risk is medium because no user-controlled command construction or network exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Home Configuration Read
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill checks $HOME/.claude-mem/settings.json and parses ~/.claude-mem/settings.json. This accesses a hidden user-level configuration file outside the project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path access is directly present in the documented command. The impact is limited because the code only checks a mode value and shows no network transmission.

low_findings.title: Command Substitution Appears Bounded
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for command injection: command substitution is used with fixed wc and cat pipelines on named files. No evidence found of user-controlled shell input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions contain fixed commands and fixed file paths. They remain operationally relevant but do not show injection behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Entropy Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: No evidence found of cryptographic code, encoded payloads, or obfuscated executable content. The cited area is normal YAML front matter and description text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found metadata only. The file is readable Markdown with Japanese and English text, not binary or encrypted content.

low_findings.title: Standard Error Redirection Is Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The /dev/null references are standard error redirections that suppress missing-file messages. No evidence found of sensitive device-file reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only device access is 2>/dev/null after cat and head. This is a common shell pattern for quiet fallback behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs execution of local shell commands to inspect git status, file sizes, and project documents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown in Bash blocks. They are not malicious by themselves, but they require command execution privileges.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads a hidden Claude-mem settings file under the user's home directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path is explicitly referenced and outside the current project. Review found no outbound transfer of the data.

content.value_statement: Starting work often requires checking branch state, task lists, and project rules. This skill gathers that context and presents a concise next-action overview.
actual_capabilities: Checks current git branch and recent commit context.
Reads Plans.md to identify active and requested tasks.
Reads AGENTS.md to summarize project constraints.
Warns when Plans.md or session-log.md may need cleanup.
Optionally detects Claude-mem harness mode and summarizes prior context.
limitations: Requires Bash permission to run the documented status commands.
Depends on local project files such as Plans.md and AGENTS.md.
Does not implement tasks or modify code by itself.
May read a hidden Claude-mem settings file when that integration exists.

use_cases.title: Resume Daily Development
use_cases.description: Review branch status, open tasks, and project rules before choosing the first implementation step.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Assigned Work
use_cases.description: Find PM-requested and in-progress tasks from Plans.md before starting a shared workflow.

use_cases.title: Recover Project Context
use_cases.description: Use git history, task files, and optional memory summaries to understand what happened before.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Start session and tell me what I should work on first.

prompt_templates.title: Check Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the current branch, task files, and project rules before I begin work.

prompt_templates.title: Resume Interrupted Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review Plans.md, AGENTS.md, and recent git history. Suggest the safest next action.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Team Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise startup report with priority tasks, constraints, risks, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.output: Current branch, priority tasks, important constraints, and the recommended first action are summarized.
The response identifies active tasks from Plans.md and suggests one focused next step.
The response combines git status, project instructions, and cleanup warnings into a short readiness report.

best_practices: Run it at the start of a work session before editing files.
Keep Plans.md and AGENTS.md current so the report is accurate.
Review any Bash or hidden-file access warning before enabling the skill.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for project-specific onboarding.
Do not rely on it when task files are missing or outdated.
Do not grant Bash access in environments that prohibit local command execution.

faq.question: Does this skill write files?
faq.answer: The documented workflow focuses on reading status and task files. It does not require file writes for normal startup reporting.

faq.question: Why does it need Bash?
faq.answer: It uses shell commands such as git status, git log, wc, cat, and head to inspect local project state.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No network access was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: What local files does it inspect?
faq.answer: It checks Plans.md, AGENTS.md, git metadata, session-log.md, and optional Claude-mem settings.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution because it can summarize local branch state and task files. Review output before sharing it externally.

faq.question: Can it decide what to implement next?
faq.answer: It can suggest a next action from task status, but the user should confirm priorities.
